prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene was performed using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth et al., JCTC 2019), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). The N−1 (cation) state used charge +1, multiplicity 2; the N+1 (anion) state used charge −1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme under GFN2-xTB. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms: Fe, 10 C, 10 H). No solvent was applied (gas phase).

Headline result: The iron center (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.25938191) site, with a negative dual descriptor (Δf = −0.030527), indicating net nucleophilic character at Fe. All Cp carbon atoms show small symmetric Fukui values (~0.0136) and all H atoms ~0.0635, reflecting the D₅h symmetry of ferrocene. The reported headline value is f⁻(Fe) = 0.25938191 (most nucleophilic site).

Integrity: status = ok, trustworthy = true. sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 — both conservation checks passed. No warnings reported by the engine.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1
Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-182654_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
